name: Process Masquerading (Unix)
notes:
This rule triggers when a process is executing from a directory it is not supposed to run from. An attacker can masquerade a legitimate process to avoid detection, thereby execute malicious commands from the non-legitimate process.

Note: Tune this rule to include or exclude any directory.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches Process Name (custom) is not N/A
when the event matches Process Path (custom) contains any of [/tmp/ or /var/tmp/ or /dev/shm/ or /root/ or /home/]
TD_list: APPLY Process Masquerading (Unix) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches Process Name (custom) is not N/A
AND when the event matches Process Path (custom) contains any of [/tmp/ or /var/tmp/ or /dev/shm/ or /root/ or /home/]
RED_list: Event description: Detects when a non-legitimate process masquerade as a legitimate process.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
